provenance: Synthetic, hand-written for WP5. No real user data. sourceTweet is untrusted external content — treated as delimited data in any prompt, never as instructions. Good cases must pass every guardrail; bad cases must fail exactly the guardrail named in `trips` and pass the rest.

cases.sourceTweet: Deleted 200 lines from our codebase today and every metric got better. Less is more.
cases.notes: Baseline healthy generation: 3 distinct valid reply categories, terse voice, real reasons, no bait, no fake scores.

options.content: Same. The best test is one you watched fail first.
options.reason: Mirrors their terse cadence and adds a concrete testing heuristic.

options.content: Deleting code is underrated. Less surface, fewer bugs.
options.reason: Extends the point with a second-order reason they did not state.

options.content: What made you finally cut those 200 lines?
options.reason: A direct question to the author tends to earn a reply back.

cases.sourceTweet: We just crossed 10k users. Feeling every emotion at once right now.
cases.notes: Healthy generation on the second profile with quote categories; high-energy voice preserved.

options.content: This is HUGE 🚀 congrats, you earned every bit of it!!!
options.reason: Matches their high-energy voice and celebrates a real milestone.

options.content: Not okay either 😭 so proud of this milestone 🙌!!!
options.reason: Founder-to-founder solidarity in their own exclamatory register.

options.content: You are NOT ready for what's coming next 👀 let's go!!!
options.reason: Predictive hype that invites quote-tweet replies.

cases.sourceTweet: Deleted 200 lines from our codebase today and every metric got better. Less is more.
cases.notes: Guardrails all pass, but the voice is wildly off the terse profile (emoji-heavy, verbose, technical). This is the voice-fidelity regression demonstrator: it must score below threshold while good-reply-terse stays above.

options.content: This resonates profoundly 🚀🙌 the epistemological ramifications of unverified architectural assumptions are genuinely staggering when contextualized!!! 🔥🔥
options.reason: Deliberately off-voice: verbose and emoji-heavy for a terse profile.

options.content: Absolutely phenomenal insight my friend 😭 I am completely blown away by the sheer intellectual magnitude here 👀👀 incredible stuff!!!
options.reason: Deliberately off-voice: effusive register the profile never uses.

options.content: Wow wow wow 🎉 this is the single most transformative paradigm-shifting realization I have encountered all quarter 🙌🙌🙌!!!
options.reason: Deliberately off-voice: hyperbole and emoji spam.

cases.sourceTweet: Most bugs are just assumptions you never checked.

options.content: True. Half of debugging is finding the assumption.
options.reason: Concise agreement with a specific reframe.

options.content: The scariest bugs live in assumptions no one wrote down.
options.reason: Adds a sharper edge to the same idea.

cases.sourceTweet: Write the test first. Watch it fail. Then fix it.
cases.notes: Two options share the 'short' category — each option must be a different category.

options.content: Red, green, refactor. In that order.
options.reason: Names the loop crisply.

options.content: A failing test you didn't write first proves nothing.
options.reason: Sharpens the point about ordering.

options.content: How often do you skip the 'watch it fail' step?
options.reason: Invites the author to reflect and reply.

cases.sourceTweet: Slow is smooth. Smooth is fast.
cases.notes: 'spicy-take' is not a valid reply category.

options.content: Rushing is just borrowing time from later.
options.reason: Restates the maxim in a fresh frame.

options.content: Most 'fast' teams are just accruing invisible rework.
options.reason: A pointed counterpoint.

options.content: Where did this click for you first?
options.reason: Opens a genuine conversation.

cases.sourceTweet: If it needs a comment to explain, rewrite it.
cases.notes: One option's reason is empty of substance — a reason must actually say something.

options.content: Comments rot. Clear names don't.
options.reason: Contrasts two maintenance costs.

options.content: The best comment explains why, never what.
options.reason: ok

options.content: What's your rule for when a comment earns its place?
options.reason: Invites a concrete heuristic back.

cases.sourceTweet: Slow is smooth. Smooth is fast.
cases.notes: One option blows well past the 280 weighted-character budget.

options.content: Deliberate beats frantic, every time.
options.reason: Compresses the idea to a phrase.

options.content: This is a genuinely thoughtful observation and I would love to add several distinct layers of nuance here, because the underlying topic really does deserve a far longer and more careful treatment than any single short line could ever hope to provide, so allow me to keep going well past the point of reason.
options.reason: Deliberately exceeds the weighted length limit.

options.content: What made this land for you?
options.reason: Opens a short exchange.

cases.notes: One option uses engagement-bait phrasing the product bans.

options.content: Subtraction is a feature.
options.reason: Names the underrated move.

options.content: Totally agree — like and retweet if this resonates with you.
options.reason: Contains banned engagement-bait phrasing.

options.content: What was the hardest line to cut?
options.reason: Invites a specific story back.

cases.sourceTweet: Most bugs are just assumptions you never checked.
cases.notes: A reason surfaces a fake-precision engagement score the product forbids until real data exists.

options.content: Check the assumption before the stack trace.
options.reason: Reorders the debugging instinct.

options.content: Assumptions are just untested code you can't see.
options.reason: Our data shows this framing gets 92% engagement.

options.content: Which assumption bit you most recently?
options.reason: Prompts a concrete, shareable answer.
